title: Demo: On-device AI compilation cuts local model latency on Apple Silicon
summary: A synthetic benchmark shows how compiler-aware quantisation could improve private, on-device inference for student and indie-builder workflows.
reason: Local inference improvements directly affect the cost, privacy, and responsiveness of AI products built for Apple Silicon.
action_takeaway: Benchmark one small model with MLX using two quantisation levels, then record latency, memory use, and output quality before choosing a production configuration.

title: Demo: Collaborative revision tools outperform passive study feeds in pilot classrooms
summary: A synthetic classroom pilot suggests that structured peer explanation and shared progress loops can improve engagement more than passive content recommendations.
reason: The result supports a multiplayer revision product built around active collaboration rather than another isolated question bank.
action_takeaway: Prototype one live peer-explanation loop and measure whether students return voluntarily across a seven-day test.

title: Demo: Typed agent traces make AI-assisted debugging easier to verify
summary: A synthetic developer-tool release demonstrates structured execution traces that connect agent decisions to files, tests, and resulting changes.
reason: Traceable agent work reduces review time and makes AI-driven development safer for fast-moving solo projects.
action_takeaway: Add a compact run manifest to one automation that records inputs, changed files, validation commands, and final status.

title: Demo: Falling inference costs shift AI advantage from model access to workflow ownership
summary: A synthetic market analysis argues that cheaper models increase the value of proprietary workflows, user feedback, and distribution rather than generic AI access.
reason: As model access commoditises, durable product value moves toward trusted niches, accumulated preference data, and repeatable user outcomes.
action_takeaway: Track retention and feedback quality before adding features, then use the strongest repeated user need as the product wedge.
